body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Deadline Aware Streams in QUIC Multipath
SCION Deployment and Internet Draft Updates

Tony Johan from the netsis lab at the University of maurg and now I'd like to present our work on dntp a deadline multipath transfer protocol that addresses the needs for realtime applications um more than realtime applications such as uh tele operation T surgery or even online gaming have three critical requirements from the netor work those are strict latency guarantees High reliability and predictable performance currently in cases where these requirements are really strict uh we use dedicated networks or lease lines uh these are very effective but comes with significant drawbacks namely high costs limited flexibility and high complex highly complex management overhead this is where s comes in uh uh as a Next Generation internet architecture San offers pathaway networking and multipath support this opens up uh new possibilities for optimizing realtime applications at the network level uh through our proposed uh protocol dntp the primary goal of the MTP is to meet the strict latency requirements while ensuring reliable data delivery utilizing science path awareness we achieve this through Three core components these are optimal path selection smart retransmission and adaptive forward data correction additionally we also support multiple concurrence streams with different priorities making it suitable for complex applications with varying requirements from the network the first core component of the ntp is the optimal path selection where we uh take multiple path metrics such as rtts uh the available bandwidth loss rate and path costs and we feed into the optimizer and Optimizer goal is to meet the application deadlines and bandwidth requirements while minimizing the overall transmission cost we uh employ a high hybrid approach combining fast htic based filtering and advanced advanced optimization algorithms the huris stics based filters quickly filter paths that can't meet deadline that can't meet dead lines and allocates traffic proportionally over the selected paths based on V metrics running in parallel to this approach is uh an advanced Optimizer we have tried using linear programming and multiobjective optimization and these solvers find truly optimal set of paths to use uh the solutions from these Advanced algorithms then feed into the htics filters and tune their weights and allowing our system to make quick decisions while converging to Optimal Behavior the second core component is M Transmission here in practice we use uh a faster path among the selected paths for acknowledgements and retransmissions and we make uh retransmission Decisions by calculating if we can still meet deadlines and also check if we have enough acknowledged packets uh for reconstruction through error correction and we also consider the congestion state of the path uh this approach avoids wasting bandwidths on packets that would arrive too late and maintains predictable latency bonds for for the stream the third code component is adaptive forward era correction we use read Solomon coding it supports multiple rati recovery recovery and uh we adapt our coding rate based on observe loss patterns uh over the paths the uh redundancy package are sent via a different path than the main data path if we have multiple paths to decorrelate losses this approach reduces dependency on R Transmissions while maintaining deadline adherence when we have random losses over CL dmtp also supports multiple priorities for streams currently we have three priorities priority zero is maximum reliability this is see that streams with uh that that are really critical but low bandwidth here we can duplicate the data across multiple paths and make sure that the the uh we have maximum reliability there and for Priority One is uh aimed at streams that need High reliability and deadline adherence but are of high bandwidth requirement for example uh a critical video stream here we target High dead deadline achievement and it uses the combination of uh forward correction and R Transmissions the third priority is best effort uh these are suitable for auxiliary streams here packet will be dropped if the bandwidth is limited and uh free up Pand for the critical streams we um did experiments with the ntp over two paths one uh high lows high bandwidth path and one low loss low bandwidth path we in in in the experiment we increased uh increased the loss rate at different at varying steps the deadline was set to 160 milliseconds um and the Blue Line represents how the coding rate was adapted with the increasing gloss rates the green spikes uh show that R Transmissions happen when coding rate was being adapted and overall uh we achieved more than 99% of packets with were were sent within the deadline even at a a loss rate of 10% we also compared the ntp with uh multipath DCP and multipath quick here we uh experimented with three variants of dntp one only with forward correction one with only with uh R transmission and one where uh these approaches were combined looking at the performance curve we see three distinct regions at low loss rates all of these protocols perform similarly well uh at the loss rate between 05 and 2% mptcp begins to degrade uh about 2% uh both mptcp and MP quick struggles while dmtp maintains consistent performance even uh when the loss rate was 10% the difference stems from fundamental design choices uh mptcp and npq optimize for high throughput and high number of connections while dmtp prioritizes meeting deadlines and maintaining reliability dmtp does use more resources per packet to achieve this but this tradeoff is Justified when uh the applications are uh require strict latency um we also validated dmtp in the real world setting uh by collaborating with eth sux robotic systems lab we successfully operated an excavator remotely over dmtp uh and for this test we used the science test bed Network called San lab um there is a video showing the difference but since this one transfer well in an online presentation you can check the video from from this link so the result was that dmtp provided consistent performance for the video with uh considerably less Jeter and uh the operator had had a good feel of how how the excavator was moving compared to uh using RTP over the regular internet so we are actively working on standardization uh through our uh experimental draft the quick multipath dntp uh we which uh we propose minimal extensions to quick and quick multipath to uh add support for deadline aare streams the key components of our proposals proposal is uh first transport parameter for negotiating support for deadline a streams a control frame to Signal per per stream deadlines uh partial reliability dntp the primary goal of the MTP is to
meet the strict latency requirements
while ensuring reliable data delivery
utilizing science path
awareness we achieve this through Three
core components these are optimal path
selection smart retransmission and
adaptive forward data
correction additionally we also support
multiple concurrence streams with
different priorities making it suitable
for complex applications with varying
requirements from the network
the first core component of the ntp is
the optimal path
selection where we uh take multiple path
metrics such as rtts uh the available
bandwidth loss rate and path costs and
we feed into the optimizer and Optimizer
goal is to meet the application
deadlines and bandwidth requirements
while minimizing the overall
transmission
cost we uh employ a high hybrid approach
combining fast htic based filtering and
advanced advanced optimization
algorithms the huris stics based filters
quickly filter paths that can't meet
deadline that can't meet dead lines and
allocates traffic proportionally over
the selected paths based on V
metrics running in parallel to this
approach is uh an advanced Optimizer we
have tried using linear programming and
multiobjective optimization and these
solvers find truly optimal set of paths
to
use uh the solutions from these Advanced
algorithms then feed into the htics
filters and tune their weights and
allowing our system to make quick
decisions while converging to Optimal
Behavior the second core component is M
Transmission here in practice we use uh
a faster path among the selected paths
for acknowledgements and
retransmissions and we make uh
retransmission Decisions by calculating
if we can still meet deadlines and also
check if we have enough acknowledged
packets uh for reconstruction through
error correction and we also consider
the congestion state of the
path uh this approach avoids wasting
bandwidths on packets that would arrive
too late and maintains predictable
latency bonds for for the
stream the third code component is
adaptive forward era
correction we use read Solomon coding it
supports multiple rati recovery
recovery and uh we adapt our coding rate
based on observe loss patterns uh over
the
paths the uh redundancy package are sent
via a different path than the main data
path if we have multiple paths to
decorrelate
losses this approach reduces dependency
on R Transmissions while maintaining
deadline adherence when we have random
losses over
CL dmtp also supports multiple
priorities for streams currently we have
three priorities priority zero is
maximum reliability this is see that
streams with uh that that are really
critical but low bandwidth here we can
duplicate the data across multiple
paths and make sure that the the uh we
have maximum reliability
there and for Priority One is uh aimed
at streams that need High reliability

for example uh a critical video stream
